accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and accurate context (FDA approval, PHSA Section 546, funding figures) is verified.

no_hallucinations.why: The FDA attribution and overdose-reversal description are correct context, not fabrications.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill reauthorizes and expands the first responder training grant program.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Identifies that the bill amends Section 546 of the Public Health Service Act and describes the textual changes.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions, conditions, or carve-outs to note.

effective_dates.why: Notes the new FY2026-2030 funding period and that no separate effective date is specified.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the scope expansion, terminology changes, funding increase, and technical correction.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names Section 546 of the Public Health Service Act and the FDA as the approving agency.

correct_figures.why: Both the $36,000,000 (FY2019-2023) and $57,000,000 (FY2026-2030) figures match the bill.
